title: Chapter 46 – Preparation (2)
content:
The crown prince drew closer.

Jeong Yeon-shin stood motionless, sword in hand.

Sword song. In that moment, the foundation of an art capable of handling groups had been born.

"Let's meet often."

Crown Prince Zhu Lun-ming said.

That was all. The crown prince betrayed no further emotion. He seemed to have lived that way his entire life.

'Does he mean don't die out there somewhere?'

Jeong Yeon-shin raised his head after completing his bow. By then, the crown prince had already disappeared into the armory.

Cheong Myeong sauntered over and draped an arm around Jeong Yeon-shin's shoulders.

"That's how goodwill works. It can't be earned all at once. And even goodwill isn't enough if you want the Divine Sword Squad Leader position. You need trust, built through consistency."

"That's right. Accumulating merit comes first, regardless."

Jeong Yeon-shin answered matter-of-factly.

He sheathed his sword and wore it at his waist alongside the Desolate Sword. Swords were consumables. Blades that never dulled were said to be among the rarest treasures under heaven.

The newly acquired Northern Light settled against him with a pleasant coolness.

"Go on ahead. There's somewhere I need to be."

"Alright."

Cheong Myeong answered simply and turned away.

Blue Eyes Demon Sword Cheong Myeong. Outwardly casual and friendly, he was someone who carried no lingering attachments to anything.

Jeong Yeon-shin watched his retreating figure for a moment before setting off.

Sunset was approaching, which meant the appointed time was right. At the end of twilight-bathed steps lay the Desolate Sect Lord's main fortress.

He climbed the stairs and entered without hesitation.

Since earning his blue rank, he felt completely woven into the fabric of Desolate Sect.

"Lightning Flash is—"

He was about to announce himself by the title that had become his courtesy name since the coming-of-age ceremony.

The massive stone door of the sect leader's office swung open on its own. Just like during the Desolate Exam.

Now he could detect the energy waves, however faintly.

They called it Void Grasp — the realm of touching all things without physical contact.

Was this a fragment of the martial arts that had allegedly destroyed the Dali Kingdom's royal family single-handedly?

They said the years immediately following Ming's founding were turbulent times.

Though she could have entered the imperial palace, the Desolate Sect Lord had married no one.

They said she refused even when the current empress dowager, still vigorous, made repeated overtures.

They said she simply lived as the Great Ming's divine sword, teaching the emperor's successors as grand preceptor.

The Desolate Sect Lord's heaven-shaking martial power had been forged through that life.

'I still need to increase my internal force.'

Jeong Yeon-shin thought. He imagined becoming Divine Sword Squad Leader and floating two swords through empty air.

If swords moved independently of his body while he deployed Eternal Blossom Fist, the effectiveness would be devastating.

At that level, martial power would become irrelevant to earning merit. But such a realm remained distant.

"You've grown comfortable. Having stray thoughts in my presence."

A clear voice rang out, and he turned.

The thick tree trunks piercing through the office were a strange sight no matter how many times he saw them.

A woman reclined diagonally on one of those trees.

Her presence was unchanged.

Her beauty — seeming only three or four years older than Jeong Yeon-shin — was said to be a mark of immortality.

Deep green pupils that seemed capable of turning souls to stone were overwhelming. Different from the gaze of other supreme masters.

Her long, pale green hair hung perfectly still. It meant she had completely contained her energy waves.

'And yet I can sense her level. What realm could this be?'

He greeted her first with an apology.

The Desolate Sect Lord slowly spoke.

"You seem to have made up your mind."

"Yes. I request internal energy techniques from the sect leader. Specifically, martial arts with a fast energy absorption speed."

He had come to claim the promised reward.

This was thanks to the reckless sparring challenge from Ma Se-in of the Desolate Ma Family.

A gift granted through the friendship between his maternal grandfather — the previous Divine Sword Squad Leader — and the Desolate Sect Lord. There was no reason to delay any longer.

Jeong Yeon-shin knew exactly what he lacked most.

"Internal energy techniques. Yes. True energy quantity is also important. Once you've polished the vessel, it must be filled with water."

She smiled and continued.

"Something comes to mind. Come here."

"Yes."

Jeong Yeon-shin moved without hesitation.

He had just completed his martial vessel. The Jeong Family Dynamic Technique was ideal for tempering the physical body.

But under heaven, there existed countless methods for accumulating energy. Building true energy. They called it Energy Accumulation.

'I need to create mind techniques with rapid energy accumulation.'

This wasn't something he could grasp merely by observing others' energy circulation.

The right approach was to directly experience a divine technique — to understand what principles drew the world's energy so quickly.

Afterward, he planned to create internal energy techniques perfectly suited to his own body.

"What are you doing?"

The Desolate Sect Lord asked. Apparently Jeong Yeon-shin had approached and then simply stood there, blank-faced.

"You said to come."

"Closer."

"By closer, you mean..."

At his uncertain words, the Desolate Sect Lord's lips curved slightly upward.

"This seems to be your first proper guidance in energy absorption. If training manuals alone sufficed, there would be no need for masters. Reciting old techniques is never enough."

She slowly raised herself upright and drew in her outstretched legs.

Her pale green martial clothes rustled softly against the tree bark.

She settled into a cross-legged posture, each foot resting on the opposite thigh.

"Sit before me properly. I will guide your true energy. Internal energy techniques must be learned this way — all at once."

"Yes. But before that."

Jeong Yeon-shin stepped back three paces.

He began to bow, slowly and deliberately.

It was because of what martial arts meant in the martial world. This was an era where sect honor was valued above personal advancement.

To receive martial arts that would endure through history — one could only accept her as master.

Forbidding master-disciple bonds? The sect leader stood above Desolate Sect's laws.

'She isn't stopping me.'

That seemed to signify acceptance. Jeong Yeon-shin completed one bow and rose.

This was sufficient. One does not bow more than twice to the living.

Such was the custom of the Central Plains, where Taoism had taken root. The martial world still honored it.

He sat before the Desolate Sect Lord, who wore a faint, enigmatic smile.

This close, her skin was startlingly white and translucent.

She smiled slightly.

"Not facing me. Turn around."

Her faint breath grazed Jeong Yeon-shin's cheek. An exhalation so thin it was barely perceptible.

It made him wonder just how many years of internal energy cultivation could produce such deep breathing.

Jeong Yeon-shin turned with fresh awe.

'I thought she would teach the chants first, then begin guiding true energy.'

She seemed to want to complete the transmission quickly. He recalled when he had transferred the Great Violet Pill's energy to Yu-hyeon, the Mount Hua sect leader's disciple.

Jeong Yeon-shin could circulate energy while speaking. The Desolate Sect Lord was surely capable of the same.

"It is called Moon Spirit Harmony Technique. My personal martial art."

As her voice faded, her hand came to rest on Jeong Yeon-shin's back. It was very warm.

Setting aside her youthful appearance, he wondered if this was what an unseen mother might feel like.

Boom.

Pure internal energy beyond anything he had imagined surged into his body.

A crystalline stream of energy entered as though carrying nature itself.

Every fine meridian and energy channel in his body began filling in sequence. It was a movement that revealed Moon Spirit Harmony Technique's path of energy accumulation.

"This is how it's done. Feel it."

Jeong Yeon-shin closed his eyes and let his mind settle into calm.

As the Desolate Sect Lord's voice guided him through the chants, he felt the current of true energy.

Gradually, his intent began circulating energy on its own.

"Yes. That's it."

Her low voice encouraged the flow.

Half an hour was enough.

When the Desolate Sect Lord lifted her fingertips from his back, her internal force dispersed with them.

Jeong Yeon-shin had absorbed the divine technique through a single circulation, guided by the old chants. Nothing more was needed.

A breath tinged with quiet laughter tickled the nape of his neck. Her whisper reached his ear.

"Come to me if there are blockages."

"Thank you for the reward."

Jeong Yeon-shin descended, turned, and offered a respectful clasped-fist greeting. She smiled once more.

On the path back to the Radiant Demon Wing pavilion after leaving the office.

His heart was soaring. Jeong Yeon-shin was struck anew by how vast the world of martial arts truly was.

This was something entirely different from the true energy operation of sword and fist-leg techniques.

He truly felt that supreme martial arts existed even in the methods of drawing nature's energy into the body and containing it there.

Was it the sect leader's consideration? Moon Spirit Harmony Technique didn't reject the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique's energy.

Like her own energy, which seemed to embrace all things.

"Very good."

The words slipped out unbidden. It felt as though inspiration was surging from every direction.

He was certain he could create mind techniques far surpassing the third-rate level immediately.

'I want to see Se-in.'

He found himself deeply grateful to his distant relative Ma Se-in and to his inadequate maternal grandfather, Ma Yeon-jeok.

* * *

Jeong Yeon-shin devoted himself to mind technique training and creation until the following evening.

It was the first time since childhood that he had sat beneath his bed, refining internal force.

Focusing solely on the living, breathing energy flowing inside and outside his body proved more agreeable than expected.

He had just completed one major heavenly circuit of full-body circulation when Do Yu-won from the General Administration Office arrived at his room.

"This is truly a matter for congratulations. Another direct disciple of the sect leader has been born."

Do Yu-won said with a bright smile. He carried two sets of blue clothes draped over his arm.

"Those are...?"

"Though you received new martial uniforms just the day before yesterday, you must wear different ones now."

Do Yu-won carefully unfolded one set of clothes.

It was a martial uniform. There wasn't much difference from the blue uniform the Desolate Sect Lord had given him at the promotion ceremony.

Only the color of the 'Hwang(荒)' character embroidered on both shoulders and back was different.

'Hwang(荒)' was stitched in golden thread.

T/Note- In case, if you have forgotten, Hwang means Desolate.

"Those who receive the sect leader's personal instruction carry a certain symbolism, regardless of uniform color. They're destined to become famous in the martial world. Therefore, Sir Jeong should also receive the proper ceremony from the main fortress, and the General Administration Office has prepared accordingly."

"I see. Thank you."

Jeong Yeon-shin accepted the blue martial uniform with golden thread.

At the same time, Do Yu-won made an "oh, that reminds me" expression and added:

"Actually, we were watching His Highness the crown prince, who already receives instruction. Though the sect leader doesn't mind such things, for people in our position it's different. But somehow, they say he let it pass without difficulty."

Jeong Yeon-shin nodded calmly and saw him off.

He changed clothes immediately and left the room. Ma Jin had summoned him.

The golden characters were strikingly visible.

Seniors he passed in the corridors widened their eyes.

Brushing off the attention with the explanation that it had simply happened that way, he arrived at the main training ground, where Radiant Demon First Team and Ma Jin had already assembled.

Junior Shin So-bin, newly assigned to First Team, was also present.

"S-Sir! What is that glorious golden splendor?!"

After Jeong Yeon-shin had weathered Hyeon Won-chang's outburst, Ma Jin spoke.

"I'll keep this brief. Since everyone agreed to short mission cycles. First, under blue-level Lightning Flash's command, Hyeon Won-chang and Shin So-bin will set out on the initial phase. You three are the right fit. This task requires young faces."

"Young faces?"

Jeong Yeon-shin asked quietly. He was suddenly reminded of Shaolin's Little Divine Monk, who had spoken of young martial artists' romance.

"There's a family that people call a righteous martial house. You've probably heard of them. The Hwangbo family."

Ma Jin said. He twisted the scar at his mouth corner.

"The truth is different. The Azure Sky Squad uncovered their secret long ago. They've been conscripting commoners to build their family's new base. Without pay, without even allowing them to tend their own livelihoods. They're forcing labor not sanctioned by the imperial court. Upon the people."

"Despicable b*st*rds!"

"It's common."

Hyeon Won-chang's outrage and Shin So-bin's cold assessment formed a sharp contrast.

Major sect martial artists were superhumans. In civilian life, they wielded martial power that made them practically gods.

With agents they could command with a snap of their fingers spread everywhere, some inevitably abused that power.

"The forced laborers number over a thousand. Civilian livelihood directly tied to tribute is a deeply sensitive matter. There's a limit to how long we can look the other way when it comes to local martial world noble clans. But since punishing orthodox sects requires careful handling, public sentiment and perception must fully support Desolate Sect's hand..."

Ma Jin trailed off briefly before continuing.

"They completely dominate Jinan territory in Shandong. Already behaving like royalty. There's a limit to simply exposing their conduct. Lightning Flash. Your role is critical."

"What's the mission?"

"Conceal your identity and enter the Dragon Phoenix Gathering with two white-level warriors. This particular Gathering happens to be held in South Zhili under the Namgung family's supervision. Since Shandong is directly above it, the Hwangbo family's young master will be there too."

Ma Jin paused briefly, then spoke again, and at last Jeong Yeon-shin's fourth mission took shape.

"Your mission is provocation. Get under his skin. Drive the young master to the breaking point. Force him to mobilize his family's power to eliminate you. You need the Hwangbo family's forces to move in strength — enough to tear open gaps in the grip they hold over all of Jinan."

"And if I succeed?"

"While you're in South Zhili, we'll immediately extract reliable witnesses and evidence. This has been in preparation for a long time. Three of the Divine Sword Squad's Seventeen Squads will converge. All of Radiant Demon Wing, together with the Azure Sky Squad and Annihilation Team, will strike the Hwangbo family."

It meant annihilating one pillar of the orthodox murim's Eight Great Families.

The scale was staggering. The coordination of three Divine Sword Squad martial squads only underscored it.

With thirteen major evil sect branches in the Central Plains alone, seventeen squads were necessary for missions spanning the vast world.

When three of them gathered, they could be called invincible.

'Incredible...!'

Jeong Yeon-shin felt a tremor run through him. Hyeon Won-chang and Shin So-bin looked equally wide-eyed.

He spoke with the most composed expression he could manage. Because there was one essential question remaining.

"About the Hwangbo family's young master. If circumstances aren't favorable, may I take his head?"

"That's left to a blue warrior's discretion. That's what blue-level rank means. You're authorized to take responsibility for whatever happens."

Ma Jin answered with a smile. Baek Mi-ryeo beside him spoke, concern etched on her face.

"Lightning Flash. Understand that this is a very dangerous mission. Don't push yourself too hard. That young master — he's fated to die regardless. He was born with a short-lived constitution, the price for his tremendous talent. He's already famous for it."

Cheong Myeong added:

"They say he's a genius of the Solar Divine Meridian. He'll be quite strong."
